mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL reflects persistent inflation that erodes real earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, labor market deterioration triggers risk-off repositioning in equities, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Following UNRATE increases greater than 0.3pp, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising FEDFUNDS and falling INDPRO indicates a restrictive monetary policy hitting real output, a combination that sharply depresses corporate profit forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score combination of FEDFUNDS and INDPRO produces a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the unconditional baseline by less than 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4 percent, a month-over-month increase in housing starts demonstrates that construction demand remains robust despite restrictive financing conditions, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because markets revise recession probability downward.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with FEDFUNDS above 4%
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4%, month-over-month HOUST increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and consumption capacity, which drives earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls by more than 1% over two consecutive months, a supply-side contraction raises inventory destocking fears and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 1% over two consecutive months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL alongside declining UNRATE signals an overheating economy that pressures the Federal Reserve to accelerate tightening, a combination that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the unconditional negative return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects aggregate demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth across sectors, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.
